title: Behen oleraceum
intro: Behen oleraceum, also known as Borage, is an annual herbaceous plant belonging to the family Boraginaceae. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to improve heart health and cognitive function. The bioactive constituents of Borage include gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), a polyunsaturated fatty acid, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids. Borage is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and capsules, as well as being incorporated into skincare products due to its high oil content.
taxonomy: Behen oleraceum is a species of plant that belongs to the family Brassicaceae. It is a type of wild cabbage or oilseed crop. The plant is further classified as belonging to the genus Camellia in the family Theaceae, however it is also sometimes classified as belonging to the genus Behen. Some sources group Behen oleraceum as part of the genus Camellia, however this is not universally agreed upon. Further research is needed to confirm the exact classification of Behen oleraceum.
